items.summary: Pack your Ranzen, say what school items you need, and hear what teachers teach.
Say how you find each school subject, then listen for which subjects Patrick likes.
React to TV shows: sort them by preference and describe each with adjectives.
Write what's on TV from a schedule, then listen and complete the programme table.
Answer questions about your TV habits, then read three teens' views on television.
Tell the time: match clocks to phrases, listen for exact times, build separable verbs.
Write sentences about your morning and describe Marie Claire's full daily schedule.
Answer questions about your day, then read a text about Marsi the Martian.
Comprehension questions on the Marsi text, then complete a daily-routine dialogue.
Grammar: conjugate irregular present-tense verbs essen, nehmen, geben, sehen, fahren.
Grammar: separable-verb conjugation, build sentences, identify subject vs. object (accusative).
Grammar: nominative vs. accusative and the accusative articles einen/ein/eine, den/die/das.
Grammar: accusative articles, accusative pronouns ihn/sie/es, and directional in + accusative.
Grammar: answer with negation using kein and nicht (food, school items, opinions).
Grammar: temporal prepositions um/am and forming time questions (Wann, Wie lange).
Listen-and-respond speaking drills (food, school, TV, times) plus intonation repetition practice.
Memory game: match food and drink picture-cards with their sentence-completion word cards.
Reverse side of the p.89 memory cards, all labelled 'Wortschatz: Essen und Trinken'.
